issues.detail: All annotations are in English (form/lemma from translation text: 'square', 'has', 'four', 'right', 'angles', etc.). LLM annotated the English translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate German: Quadrat (noun), Winkel (noun), achsen- and punktsymmetrisch (adjectives).

Only annotation: form='Sie', lemma='sie', pos='noun', gloss_hint='trumpet solo'. Three problems: (1) 'Sie' is a pronoun, not a noun; (2) gloss_hint 'trumpet solo' is completely wrong; (3) many content words are missing annotations: sternförmig (adjective), regelmäßigen (adjective), Quadraten (noun), verschiedene (adjective), Muster (noun), zeigten (verb).
form='Quadrat', lemma='quad' — lemma should be 'Quadrat', not 'quad' (English/abbreviation).

form='zum', lemma='zu', pos='adjective' — 'zum' is a preposition/contraction, not adjective. Also form='gleich' annotated as adjective; here 'gleich' functions as a mathematical equals sign (adverb/particle), though the annotation is borderline acceptable.

form='Deutscher', lemma='German' — lemma is English, should be 'deutsch' (adjective) or 'Deutscher' (noun, ethnic German). Also form='Rap', lemma='rap' — lemma should be capitalized 'Rap' (German noun).

form='Ich', lemma='ich', pos='noun' — should be pos='pronoun'. form='meinen', lemma='mein', pos='verb' — 'meinen' here is the declined possessive pronoun 'mein' (meinen Sohn), not the verb meinen; lemma='mein', pos='pronoun'.

form='joggt', lemma='joggen', pos='verb' — the sentence text has 'joggte' (past tense), so form should be 'joggte', not 'joggt'. Also form='jeden', lemma='jeden', pos='adjective' — 'jeden' is a determiner (accusative of 'jeder'), not adjective; lemma should be 'jeder'.

form='ablegen', lemma='ablegen', pos='verb', gloss_hint='pass (exam)' — gloss_hint is wrong. This is 'einen Schwur ablegen' = to take/swear an oath. The relevant gloss_en for ablegen in this context would be something like 'take (oath)' or null.

form='Nach', lemma='nach', pos='adverb' — 'nach' here is a preposition ('Nach kurzer Messe'), not adverb; pos should be 'preposition'. form='kurzer', lemma='kurz', pos='noun' — should be adjective, not noun.

All annotations have English lemmas ('the', 'total', 'show', 'which', 'Ganzes') with gloss_hint='>'. LLM annotated an old/incorrect version. The German text 'Die Totale zeigt das Ganze' should have annotations: Totale (noun, lemma='Totale'), zeigt (verb, lemma='zeigen'), Ganze (noun, lemma='Ganze'). Current annotations should be replaced.

Translation says 'drain the pasta' but 'abschrecken' in cooking means to rinse/quench with cold water after cooking (shock-cooling), not drain. Better: 'Now I'll briefly rinse the pasta with cold water, and then we can eat.' The word 'abschrecken' also appears in text with its prefix 'ab' — lemma annotation for verb form 'schrecke...ab' is split awkwardly.

Translation 'I have never felt it and do not regret it' is wrong. German 'Ich habe nie gefohlt' = 'I have never foaled'. The text is narrated from a mare's perspective. Correct: 'I have never foaled and do not regret it.' The rest of the translation is also inaccurate ('thrown into the passionate pussy' is a mistranslation; 'Muschik' is a Russian word for peasant/muzhik, and 'geilen Muschik' here means something like 'lustful/horny male'). Also annotation has form='gefühlt' (felt) instead of the actual form 'gefohlt' (foaled).

Annotations are entirely in English (form='last', lemma='last'; form='tip', lemma='Tipp'; form='Vegetarians'; form='and'; form='vegans'; form='must'; form='to'; form='eat'; form='French'; form='fries'). LLM annotated the English translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate the German: letzter (adjective), Hinweis (noun), Vegetarier (noun), Veganer (noun), müssen (verb), ernähren (verb), Pommes (noun).

Translation 'Their votes did not come through' is wrong. 'Stimmen' here means 'voices' (not 'votes'), and 'drangen nicht durch' = 'did not get through / penetrate'. Better: 'Their voices did not get through, no matter how hard they tried.' Also annotation form='drangen', lemma='dringen', gloss_hint='vote' — gloss_hint 'vote' is wrong, should be null or a hint about this sense.

Translation only renders the second sentence ('She certainly does not want to give herself up for nothing') but ignores the first sentence ('Wenn es ihr nicht gelinge, prominent zu bleiben, dann müsse sie das einsehen' = 'If she did not succeed in staying prominent, then she would have to accept that'). Full translation needed.

Annotations are entirely in English (form='The', lemma='the'; form='women', lemma='woman'; form='hurried', lemma='hurry'; etc.). LLM annotated the English translation instead of the German text. Should annotate German: Weiber (noun), drängten (verb), herbei (adverb), mühten (verb), sich (pronoun), lösten (verb), brachten (verb), Ohnmächtigen (noun), Riechfläschchen (noun), Nase (noun).

form='das', lemma='which' — lemma should be German 'das', not English 'which'. form='Transporter', lemma='transporter' — lemma should be capitalized German 'Transporter'. form='praktizieren', gloss_hint='practice' — this example uses the 'maneuver/skillfully move' sense, so gloss_hint='practice' doesn't match the right sense (should be 'maneuver' or null).

Translation 'practicing as a carpenter' is misleading — 'praktizieren' in 'praktiziere ich erfolgreich als Tischler' means working/operating as a carpenter (running one's craft), not the sense 'intern' (Praktikum). This example is better matched to the 'practice' sense (Sense 1: ausüben) or the 'practice medicine' sense (Sense 3: den Beruf ausüben). It belongs under sense 3 generalized or sense 1, not sense 4 (intern). Translation is acceptable but the sense assignment in the word file may be incorrect.
